title: Lappula cynoglossoides
taxonomy: Lappula cynoglossoides is a species of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae). It belongs to the genus Lappula, which is a group of plants that are native to the temperate and alpine regions of the Northern Hemisphere. Lappula cynoglossoides is a perennial herb with small blue flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant's classification is further categorized under the family Boraginaceae and the order Borales. Within the Boraginaceae family, Lappula cynoglossoides is often placed in the subfamily Lappuloideae.

intro: Lappula cynoglossoides, also known as the forked bluebells or forked-horned prickly poppy, is a perennial plant species belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, respiratory issues, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Lappula cynoglossoides include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Lappula cynoglossoides include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to alleviate colds, coughs, and sore throats.
